pre_bet_model_projection.edge_classification: strong positive EV after sample-size correction

athletics_offense_vs_mcdonald.notable_contact_profile: groundball-heavy suppression; 9 groundouts, 0 flyouts recorded against McDonald

liveflow_timeline.read: Initial market looked volatile due to 2-game MLB sample; position required larger 2025 profile correction.
2025 larger-sample profile supported real K sustainability and starter leash; projection upgraded to mean 5.3 / median 5.

liveflow_timeline.line: 5.0 IP, 4 H, 1 ER, 1 BB, 4 K, 69 pitches
liveflow_timeline.read: Ticket needed 1 more K; runway remained alive due to low pitch count, run prevention, and leash stability.

liveflow_timeline.line: 6.2 IP, 5 H, 1 ER, 1 BB, 5 K, 90 pitches
liveflow_timeline.read: Leash projection confirmed. McDonald earned enough runway to clear the 5+ K ticket despite modest K pace.

what_worked: Corrected away from 2-game MLB sample bias
Trusted broader 2025 workload and K profile
Live runway assessment correctly identified that 69 pitches through 5.0 IP was not a hook-risk state
Contact suppression and groundball efficiency extended leash
what_to_modify: Separate dominance-based K edges from leash-based K edges in future outputs
Add explicit Sample Size Trap warning when app data is under 3 MLB starts
For low-whiff but efficient pitchers, project K hit probability as leash-dependent rather than whiff-dependent
model_evaluation.patch_recommendation: KEEP and formalize SAMPLE_SIZE_CORRECTION + LEASH_DEPENDENT_K_OVER logic

notes: This was not a pure whiff-dominance ticket. It was a corrected-profile, price-driven, leash-stability K edge. The 5+ hit came from starter runway and contact suppression rather than explosive strikeout pace. Log as a successful anti-burn correction and positive EV execution.
